Most discussions about AI agents focus on models, prompts, or frameworks. But the real intelligence of an agent often lives outside the LLM.
Whether you’re building an AI coding assistant, research agent, automation bot, or customer support system, the core execution loop is surprisingly small.
In fact, you can build a functional agent in roughly 100 lines of TypeScript without relying on heavyweight frameworks.
The secret isn’t the model call.
It’s the three engineering decisions your runtime (or harness) makes around that call.
Let’s explore them.
A Minimal Agent Loop
At its simplest, an agent repeatedly performs these steps:

• Send the conversation to the model.

• Check whether the model wants to use tools.

• Execute requested tools.

• Return tool results.

• Repeat until finished or until a maximum iteration count is reached.
Here’s a simplified implementation.
